behaviors.description: Required patient fields (name, date of birth, insurance ID) are validated before any downstream processing and missing fields produce a clear error listing all missing items.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: failure boundary is validator->pipeline handoff (must stop downstream calls), not just local field checks. REAL-INFRA/LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: none. Rejection-gate: Unit rejected because behavior text says "before any downstream processing," which requires wiring verification across modules.
behaviors.test_description: Run intake orchestrator with missing fields; assert error contains all missing field names and assert insurance/triage/scheduling/confirmation modules are not invoked.

behaviors.description: Date of birth validation rejects future dates, dates more than 150 years ago, and non-date strings.
behaviors.justification: Local Unit escape applies: single local validation policy/algorithm, no module handoff required by behavior, no real infra, no AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Table-driven tests for DOB parser/validator over valid/invalid boundary cases (future, >150 years old, malformed strings).

behaviors.description: The insurance eligibility check sends the correct patient ID and plan code to the payer API and correctly interprets `eligible` vs `not eligible` vs `pending`.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: behavior is explicitly about sending to payer API; real HTTP contract/serialization failures can be hidden by in-process mocks (MOCK-HIDE). Integration brake (a)=yes. Rejection-gate: Integration rejected because text requires actual API-bound request correctness, not only internal mapping.
behaviors.test_description: Hit a real HTTP test endpoint (or sandbox payer API) through production HTTP client, verify outbound payload fields and assert response-state mapping for eligible/not eligible/pending.

behaviors.description: When the payer API is unreachable, the system marks eligibility as `pending` and continues the intake rather than blocking.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unreachable network/subprocess-level HTTP failure semantics are the core failure mode. Also checks cross-module continuation after infra failure. Integration brake (a)=yes.
behaviors.test_description: Force real connection failure (e.g., refused/timeout to payer host) and assert eligibility becomes pending and orchestration proceeds to triage/scheduling instead of aborting.

behaviors.description: Triage invocation includes the reported symptoms and configured emergency rubric in the model input payload.
behaviors.justification: IA-UA synergy: plumbing-only. Behavior text is payload-shape: "includes ... in the model input payload," not model output quality. LLM-DEP disqualified by explicit scope to invocation content.
behaviors.test_description: With stubbed model client, execute triage service and assert request payload contains symptoms and configured rubric fields exactly.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage assigns high urgency to symptom combinations that indicate emergency conditions (for example chest pain plus shortness of breath).
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: correctness depends on real model judgment/classification quality; deterministic stubs would always pass and hide failure (deterministic-assertion trap). Not Workflow: single agentic decision step.
behaviors.test_description: Run repeated live triage calls on emergency symptom sets and assert high-urgency classification rate meets threshold; track variance across runs.

behaviors.description: The triage result parser stores a clinician-readable explanation field (not only a numeric score).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is parser->persistence handoff (field may be dropped in mapping/storage). No real AI output-quality requirement; can use fixed response fixture. Rejection-gate: Agentic rejected because behavior asks storage of explanation field presence, not explanation quality.
behaviors.test_description: Feed deterministic triage response fixture with score+explanation through parser/storage path; assert persisted record includes explanation text column/value.

behaviors.description: The live AI symptom triage explanation cites case-specific symptoms and urgency rationale, not generic boilerplate.
behaviors.justification: LLM-DEP: this is output-quality/content-grounding behavior from live AI; mocks cannot reveal generic/ungrounded explanations. Not Workflow: one model output quality check.
behaviors.test_description: Run live triage on multiple symptom scenarios and evaluate explanation for scenario-specific symptom mentions plus rationale linkage; fail generic boilerplate outputs.

behaviors.description: An appointment is scheduled in the first available slot that matches the assigned urgency level: high urgency gets same-day, medium gets within 48 hours.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: scheduling policy depends on scheduler logic + calendar data access handoff. REAL-INFRA: local DB wiring sufficient; no OS/network necessity. LLM-DEP: none for this behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Seed local calendar DB with slot inventory; invoke scheduling with high/medium urgency and assert chosen slot is first qualifying one under same-day/48h policy.

behaviors.description: The scheduling step does not double-book a time slot even when two patients are processed concurrently.
behaviors.justification: Core failure is concurrent state handoff/transaction behavior across scheduler + shared calendar DB. Per level defs, local DB concurrency testing fits Integration; real external OS/network not required. Rejection-gate: System rejected because behavior does not require subprocess/real external service beyond local DB.
behaviors.test_description: Run two concurrent scheduling requests targeting same slot against local test DB with real transaction constraints; assert only one booking succeeds and no duplicate reservation exists.

behaviors.description: The confirmation message includes patient name, appointment time, provider name, and preparation instructions appropriate to visit type.
behaviors.justification: Local Unit escape: message composition/content mapping can be validated in one formatter/template boundary with fixture inputs; no required module/infra/AI interaction in behavior text.
behaviors.test_description: Call confirmation message builder with representative visit types and assert required fields and visit-type-specific prep instructions are present.

behaviors.description: Pipeline orchestration (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes correctly with deterministic stubbed triage output.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to orchestration with "deterministic stubbed triage output," disqualifying Agentic/Workflow by text. Needs multi-module handoff validation end-to-end in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Execute full orchestrator with stubbed triage and controlled insurance/calendar adapters; assert ordered stage execution, state propagation, and final confirmation output.

behaviors.description: The full live intake pipeline (validate, insurance, triage, schedule, confirm) completes for a real patient submission without manual intervention.
behaviors.justification: Includes live AI triage call in real pipeline; execution depends on real model response behavior, so Integration would hide model-dependent failures. Not Workflow: only one agentic decision stage described (no multiple agentic steps/planner-critic loop).
behaviors.test_description: Run end-to-end intake against live dependencies (payer + AI triage + calendar) with real submission and assert autonomous completion through confirmation without human fallback.
